antiReferences.name: Gym-bro protein RTDs
antiReferences.why: Too performance-led, body-optimisation focused and solo-routine oriented for a premium social drink.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision only. It is a different US energy/probiotic product and must not be treated as the same brand, category or design reference.

antiReferences.name: Clinical electrolyte and supplement mixes
antiReferences.why: Too functional, rational and utility-led; they make the drink feel like a task rather than a social order.

antiReferences.name: Meal replacement shakes
antiReferences.why: Too heavy, satiating and nutrition-first for PEP’s light, refreshing, out-of-home role.

antiReferences.name: Childish soda or juice brands
antiReferences.why: They may be fun, but they lack the grown-up premium cues needed for bars, dinners and hospitality.

audience: Social drinkers who want a better option when they are out
People choosing drinks for mood, setting, taste and aesthetic appeal
Hospitality customers at bars, restaurants, hotels, festivals, dates and rooftop events
Wellness-aware consumers who like a smart benefit but do not want supplement behaviour in social moments
brandFitRules: Pass if the main use case is an out-of-home social order: bar, dinner, event, festival, date or rooftop.
Fail if the main use case is gym recovery, solo routine, electrolyte replenishment, desk hydration or hitting macros.
Lead with flavour, refreshment, serve ritual and social feeling; keep protein as a supporting proof point.
Do not sort competitors by alcohol content. Sort by occasion, mindset and drink format.

Use cocktail, cider and spirit brands as social-design inspiration only when they help express premium ritual or going-out energy; mark them as type mismatch when their format is not close to PEP’s protein drink.
Reject any look that feels clinical, supplement-heavy, sports-performance-led or meal-replacement-like.
Do not lock PEP to one flavour family; future flavours must fit the same platform.

designPatterns: High-recognition packaging with a polished hospitality feel
Occasion-first messaging rather than nutrition-first messaging
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong: social functional beverage with a ready-to-drink role; close enough to PEP’s social protein refreshment format to act as a primary peer.
inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: It proves that a functional benefit can live inside a social-drink world when the brand leads with mood, experience and ritual instead of pure utility.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong: ready-to-drink social beverage; no protein, but very close in occasion and ordering behaviour.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Mocktails
inLineBrands.why: It sits in the same out-of-home social decision set and shows how a soft drink can feel adult, flavour-led and bar-appropriate.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Good: mixer/soft-drink format fits social drinking occasions, though PEP adds protein and should feel more ownable as a standalone order.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Ginger Ale
inLineBrands.why: It is a familiar social-drink reference in hospitality and helps anchor PEP away from gym nutrition and toward grown-up refreshment.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: Use only for site-structure inspiration if needed
Potential reference for simple product education blocks and conversion flow
Not a positioning, tone or identity reference

oneLiner: PEP is a premium, light and refreshing protein drink made to feel exciting when you order it out.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Hydration/electrolyte mix, not a social protein refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional protein direction: rational hydration and electrolyte replenishment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful for functional clarity, but too clinical and routine-led for PEP’s social, premium, out-of-home role.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: BCAA/energy drink, not PEP’s social refreshment format.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional protein direction: sports, energy and performance.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too gym and performance oriented; PEP should not feel like a pre-workout or recovery can.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Protein fusion drink, but positioned through novelty and athletic functionality rather than premium social ritual.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional protein direction: protein-led innovation and fitness adjacency.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: The protein idea is relevant, but the brand behaviour is too novelty-functional for PEP’s polished hospitality ambition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Functional hydration, not social protein refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional protein direction: vitamins, hydration and daily utility.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: More everyday functional beverage than exciting social order.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Functional/vitamin drink, not a premium social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional protein direction: health enhancement and supplementation.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too benefit-led and supplement-adjacent for PEP’s bar, dinner and festival role.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Functional protein drink; closer in product benefit than social occasion.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Juuz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional protein direction: protein-rich, sugar-free, sports-adjacent refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: The format is relevant, but the mindset is more functional and protein-first than PEP should be in this direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Prebiotic soda, not protein social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: wellness soda and everyday healthier choice.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Fun and lifestyle-aware, but the core logic is gut-health upgrade rather than out-of-home social ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Aperitif / soft drink format; socially adjacent but board role is lifestyle upgrade.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: natural, subtle, everyday premium choice.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sophisticated and useful as a light social cue, but less high-energy and less protein-relevant than PEP’s selected social direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Prebiotic soda, not protein social refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: healthier soda and digestive wellness.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Strong health-soda brand, but too wellness-benefit led for PEP’s hospitality-first ambition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Prebiotic soda, not protein social refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: trendy everyday gut-health soda.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Energetic and flavour-led, but the buying logic is more lifestyle wellness than premium social ordering.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: wellness, probiotics and everyday refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Refreshing and approachable, but too wellness/kombucha coded for PEP’s stronger social-drink platform.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Infused sparkling water, not protein social refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: relaxation, stress relief and identity-led wellness.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Cool and modern, but calmer and more everyday than the festival/hospitality energy required here.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Cocktail/mocktail line; socially adjacent but not protein refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction in the board: convenient premium drinks for parties and outdoor moments.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Close to social occasions, but it behaves more like a cocktail lifestyle brand than a new protein-based social refreshment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Spirits, wines and mocktails; not protein refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: premium mindful social choice.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It has useful premium cues, but the main category logic is classic drink replacement rather than PEP’s own light protein ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Functional soda, not protein social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: more fiz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle upgrade direction: healthier soda with fibre and wellness claims.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too wellness-functional and everyday soda oriented for the selected social hospitality direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Yaya Kombucha
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Best understood as lifestyle/craft wellness rather than the selected social direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium and refreshing, but more craft wellness and fermentation-led than PEP’s social ordering platform.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Kombucha and botanical soda, not protein drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Walker Brothers
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Best understood as lifestyle/craft wellness rather than the selected social direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Craft and botanical cues are interesting, but the core appeal is nuanced fermentation and wellness rather than high-energy social ritual.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker: spirit and mixing format rather than PEP’s light protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Lyre's
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strong social positioning fit; weaker drink-type fit.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: It is built around spirit-style serves and classic cocktail cues, while PEP should be a standalone protein refreshment people order socially.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the sense of premium ritual, adult social legitimacy, menu readiness and serve culture. Do not copy its heritage cues or spirit-category structure.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker: concentrated/mixing ritual rather than PEP’s ready protein refreshment role.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Seedlip
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strong social positioning fit; weaker drink-type fit.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: It operates like a spirit alternative and mixer component, not a light protein drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for understanding how a drink can feel sophisticated, occasion-specific and hospitality credible without leaning on sports nutrition.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker: cider format rather than protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Rekorderlig
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strong social occasion fit; weaker drink-type fit.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Its category conventions are cider-led, while PEP needs to own a refreshing protein-drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the outdoor social ease, premium casualness, flavour-led range clarity and group-occasion appeal.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker: cocktail format rather than PEP’s protein refreshment format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: On The Rocks Cocktails
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strong social occasion fit; weaker drink-type fit.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: It is a bar-cocktail proposition, while PEP should feel like its own lighter social drink ritual.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the premium ready-to-serve confidence, bar-quality cues, occasion-led presentation and concise pack hierarchy.

positioningStatement: For people who want a better drink option when they are out, PEP is a premium, light and refreshing protein drink that turns functional benefit into a fun social ritual — something you order for taste, feeling and the moment.

visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe
visual.mood: High-energy, social, polished, celebratory, refreshing, premium-but-approachable, made for out-of-home moments.
visual.photography: Show PEP in hands, on tables, at bars, dinners, festivals, rooftops and events. Prioritise people, serve moments, movement, refreshment and atmosphere. Product shots should feel desirable and menu-ready, with flavour cues broad enough to support a growing range.

voice.tone: Confident, social, playful, premium and concise. Lead with taste, refreshment and occasion; use nutrition as a supporting proof point.
